2605.02452.title: Position: How can Graphs Help Large Language Models?
2605.02452.paper_summary: With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), classic graph learning tasks have greatly benefited from LLMs, including improved encoding of textual features, more efficient construction of graphs from text, and enhanced reasoning over knowledge graphs. In this paper, we ask a complementary question: How can graphs help LLMs? We address this question from three perspectives: 1) graphs provide an up-to-date knowledge source that helps reduce LLM hallucinations, 2) graph-based prompting techniques-such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT), Tree-of-Thought (ToT), and Graph-of-Thought (GoT)-enhance LLM reasoning capabilities, and 3) integrating graphs into LLMs improves their understanding of structured data, expanding their applicability to domains such as e-commerce, code, and relational databases (RDBs). We further outlook some future directions including designing sparse LLM architectures based on graphs and brain-inspired memory systems.
2605.02452.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）的快速進步，經典的圖學習任務從LLMs中獲益良多，包括文本特徵的編碼改善、從文本中更高效地構建圖形，以及對知識圖的推理增強。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出一個互補性問題：圖形如何幫助LLMs？<lb/>我們從三個角度來探討這個問題：1）圖形提供了最新的知識來源，有助於減少LLM的幻覺，2）基於圖形的提示技術——如思維鏈（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）、思維樹（Tree-of-Thought, ToT）和思維圖（Graph-of-Thought, GoT）——增強了LLM的推理能力，3）將圖形整合進LLMs中改善了它們對結構化數據的理解，擴展了它們在電子商務、代碼和關係數據庫（RDBs）等領域的適用性。<lb/>我們進一步展望一些未來的方向，包括基於圖形設計稀疏的LLM架構和受大腦啟發的記憶系統。
2605.02452.author: Xiyuan Wang et.al.
2605.02452.authors: Xiyuan Wang, Yi Hu, Yanbo Wang, Chuan Shi, Muhan Zhang
